show: The Black Effect Podcast Network (iHeartMedia x Charlamagne Tha God)
summary: PepsiCo Beverages, with its agency partner OMD, signed on as an inaugural advertising partner of The Black Effect Podcast Network when iHeartMedia and Charlamagne Tha God launched the network in September 2020. PepsiCo's Head of Media said the deal 'connects our brands to consumers through the newer trusted medium of podcasting' and supports PepsiCo's strategic initiative to invest in Black communities and support diverse voices.
sourceTitle: Business Wire - iHeartMedia and Charlamagne Tha God Launch The Black Effect Podcast Network

show: The Black Effect Podcast Festival / Black Effect Podcast Network
summary: State Farm served as presenting sponsor of the fourth annual Black Effect Podcast Festival (April 25, 2025, Pullman Yards, Atlanta), the live-event showcase for iHeartMedia and Charlamagne Tha God's Black Effect Podcast Network, hosted by Charlamagne Tha God and DJ Envy of The Breakfast Club. State Farm returned as presenting sponsor for the 2026 festival as well.
sourceTitle: iHeartMedia press release - Fourth Annual Black Effect Podcast Festival Presented by State Farm

show: All the Smoke (Matt Barnes & Stephen Jackson)
summary: Matt Barnes and Stephen Jackson's basketball podcast All the Smoke moved to Meadowlark Media and the DraftKings Network effective January 1, 2024, with DraftKings serving as the show's exclusive sportsbook sponsor and overseeing advertising. The partnership later expanded into new verticals including All the Smoke: Fight (combat sports) and All the Smoke: Football.
outcome: At the time of the deal the show had nearly 1 million YouTube subscribers and ranked in the top 20 of Apple and Spotify audio charts, per Awful Announcing.
sourceTitle: Awful Announcing - 'All the Smoke' NBA podcasts moving to Meadowlark Media and the DraftKings Network

brand: Fidelity Investments, AT&T, Zillow, Rap Snacks, Pronghorn, BTST Services
summary: Corporate sponsors including Fidelity Investments, Rap Snacks, Zillow, Pronghorn, AT&T, and BTST Services backed InvestFest, the financial-literacy festival created by Rashad Bilal and Troy Millings of the Earn Your Leisure podcast, which bridges finance and the hip-hop community.
outcome: Over 20,000 attendees converged on the Georgia World Congress Center for the third annual event, per Yahoo Finance.
sourceTitle: Yahoo Finance - InvestFest financial festival aims to help close the racial wealth gap

summary: Nissan is a multi-year partner of The Black Effect Podcast Network, sponsoring the Black Effect Podcast Festival with its signature 'Pitch Your Podcast' experience for emerging creators, and partnering on the 'Thrill of Possibility Summit,' which brought 50 HBCU students in STEAM disciplines together with Nissan employees and Black Effect podcast talent.
sourceTitle: Nissan USA - Nissan & Black Effect Podcast Network: Powering the Future of Podcast Storytelling

brand: Nielsen (industry benchmark study, not a single brand campaign)
summary: Nielsen's Podcast Ad Effectiveness research (October 2020) found that host-read podcast ads outperform non-host-read ads across brand lift metrics, supporting the value of creator-voiced sponsorships.
outcome: 71% of respondents recalled the advertised brand after hearing a host-read podcast ad vs. 62% for non-host-read ads; host-read ads also drove a 50% average increase in purchase and recommendation intent, per Nielsen.
sourceTitle: Nielsen - Host-Read Podcast Ads Pack a Brand Recall Punch

summary: Fanatics signed a multi-year sponsorship deal with The Pivot Podcast (hosted by former NFL players Ryan Clark, Channing Crowder, and Fred Taylor) in May 2024 -- Fanatics' first-ever podcast sponsorship, giving its brands placement on the show and the hosts access to Fanatics events and its athlete network.
outcome: Announced as a multi-year agreement and Fanatics' first podcast sponsorship; the deal ended in January 2025, roughly 8 months after signing.

summary: Nissan is a flagship advertiser of iHeartMedia and Charlamagne Tha God's Black Effect Podcast Network, sponsoring the Black Effect Podcast Festival with on-site activations and partnering on the Thrill of Possibility Summit, which brought 50 HBCU students in STEAM disciplines together with Nissan employees and Black Effect podcast talent.
outcome: Network reported brand partnerships with Nissan, AT&T, State Farm, American Express, and Molson; network downloads topped 800 million by its third anniversary in 2023.

summary: Philadelphia hosts Gillie Da King and Wallo267 signed a distribution and investment deal with Barstool Sports in 2020, reportedly worth $3 million in exchange for a 50 percent ownership stake in the show, bringing the hip-hop culture podcast into Barstool's advertiser network.
outcome: Reported $3 million deal for a 50% ownership stake in 2020; the contract was later extended, with a rumored (unconfirmed) $100 million extension.

summary: Shannon Sharpe's January 2024 Katt Williams interview on Club Shay Shay became a landmark monetization case for Black-creator podcasts: in a Hollywood Reporter interview, Sharpe implied he earned around $6 million from that single episode, and the episode became the most-watched interview in YouTube history.
outcome: 68+ million YouTube views (surpassing Joe Rogan's Elon Musk interview as the most-watched interview on YouTube); Sharpe implied roughly $6M in earnings from the single episode.

summary: Edison Research's Black Podcast Listener Report 2.0 (with SXM Media and Mindshare USA, February 2023) documented that Black Americans over-index on podcast listening and act on podcast ads at higher rates than the overall U.S. audience.
outcome: 43% of the Black 18+ U.S. population listened to a podcast in the last month vs. 38% of the overall 18+ population; after hearing an ad, 61% of Black weekly podcast listeners recommended the product (vs. 49% of U.S. weekly listeners) and 52% purchased a product or service (vs. 44%).

summary: The IAB U.S. Podcast Advertising Revenue Study (prepared with PwC) shows sustained double-digit growth in U.S. podcast ad spending, driven by network expansion, deeper brand integrations, and better audience targeting.
outcome: U.S. podcast advertising revenue grew 26.4% in 2024 to more than $2.4 billion, then grew another 17.6% in 2025 to $2.862 billion -- up from $105.7 million in 2015.
